spec_match.rationale: Concept exercises the relevant API surface (fetch(, document.querySelector, translate() with runtime script (~4118 chars) wired to live controls, in line with the feature summary.

interactive.rationale: Real interactive surface — 5 buttons, 0 inputs, 0 selects, 0 ranges — wired up via event listeners.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Concept lede matches the feature-index promise on key terms (against, allowlist, build, candidate, classifies, endpoints, issues, policy, request, then).

edge_cases.rationale: No visible edge case / fallback / error handling — only the happy path.

references.rationale: ChromeStatus link is present but the page has no dedicated `references` section — the self-heal contract is fragile.

design_system.rationale: All colours come via 73 CSS-variable references, no raw hex, no button background override without a colour override, and not a static-card pattern.

runtime_evidence.rationale: Fresh Chrome DevTools MCP v152 sweep loaded the copied concept, exercised every detected control, found named controls, and reported no post-triage runtime/action failure.

conformance.rationale: A v152 suite now carries the same feature-ID/spec contract without modifying the immutable source-milestone suite.

openQuestions.title: Add explicit edge case coverage
openQuestions.detail: Pick one failure mode the spec or chromestatus motivation calls out (unsupported browser, denied permission, empty input, etc.) and surface it as a distinct demo state alongside the happy path.

openQuestions.title: Wrap references in a labelled `references` section
openQuestions.detail: Per CLAUDE.md rule #3, the chromestatus link is the self-heal anchor and should sit inside a clearly-labelled `references` section so the recovery script can find it reliably.

openQuestions.title: Manually verify against the spec text
openQuestions.detail: Reviewer-v1-subagent based the spec_match verdict on the chromestatus summary ("Connection Allowlists is a feature designed to provide explicit control over external endpoints by restricting connections initiated via the Fetch API or other web platform APIs from a document or worker.  The proposed implementation involv") because reading the full spec for every concept was out of budget. A human or follow-up agent should diff the demo's actual behaviour against https://wicg.github.io/connection-allowlists to catch anything chromestatus glossed over.

summary: Concept ships ~4118 chars of script across 5 button(s) and 0 other control(s). hits 4 pass / 1 partial / 1 fail across the six rubric criteria.
